Okay, what is the daily huddle? I get this question all the time. Okay, the daily huddle is the pulse of your business. This is where you're going to share all critical numbers. You're going to get a little bit of motivation for the day, a little rah-rah, you wanna bring some energy. And it's also a time to ask questions so that you don't disrupt each other during the rest of the day, right? As well as announcements, birthdays, anything like that. So there's a lot of different iterations of this. You can add to it, take away from it. I want you to remember that you know your business better than anyone. And so if you feel something isn't appropriate or it's unnecessary at the point you're at, then that's okay. We change our daily huddles. What it was when we had 10 people was not the same at 20, 30, 40, 50, 90, okay? It changes as your business changes. And so what you want to do is to make sure that you are utilizing it the best to what you need right now. And so what it does do, regardless of the size, is it will create the pulse, okay? People need predictability. And so for you as the owner, it might be boring for you because you already know the numbers you're sharing. You already are motivated, you don't need motivation, and you already don't have any questions for anyone, okay? This is not for you. This is for your team, okay? It's going to create stability. It's going to create predictability. The last thing anyone wants is to work for someone who's unpredictable and not reliable. So that is why it is so important to have one of these. An example of how I would run this if I had a gym, okay? And I actually did work at a gym where we had one like this, and this is kind of how it went, okay? So one, you know, it's like, hey guys, welcome. It's Wednesday, like happy Wednesday. Hope you guys are doing freaking great. Let's go ahead and share our numbers. So sales manager, let's go. And then he's going to say lead volume, number of scheduled calls, number of closes, number of no shows, total EFTs to date, right? So he's just going to crank down those numbers. And he's not looking for them on the huddle. He has them prepared and ready. Two, operations manager. All right, we're going to go through number of welcome cards that were sent, number of billing escalations from the prior day, number of Facebook posts and messages handled, attendance numbers from the classes, and any escalated issues or help needed, okay? And then lastly, trainers, confirmation of announcements made. So let them letting you know, yes, we've been announcing that we have this new internal play rolling out. Two client or class issues, as well as questions to address. So anything that may have happened in a class or with a client. Lastly, updates on new client attendance and performance, okay? And so you just go through these as efficiently as possible. And then it can be up to you. If before or after announcing the numbers we like to do after, you kind of give a little motivation. Maybe it's a quote for the day. Maybe it's sharing an example of someone implementing one of the core values. Maybe it's sharing a client's story. You want something every day that's gonna bring them back to your mission and your why? Just a little bit, okay? Because we get so caught up in the day to day in numbers, we wanna make sure that when we're delivering a lot of numbers, we balance it with delivering the why and the how behind that.
